---
title: "Bushy Aster (Symphyotrichum dumosum)"
slug: "symphyotrichum-dumosum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180817723/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Bushy Aster"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180817723/medium.jpg" alt="Bushy Aster" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/aclarke2">
aclarke2</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107501637">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=81593)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in sandhills, pine flatwoods, and disturbed sites.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its bushy, branching growth and numerous small daisy-like flowers with white to pale purple rays and yellow centers. In Florida, it is found in a variety of habitats including flatwoods, open woodlands, and disturbed areas. It typically blooms in fall and provides an important late-season nectar source for pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/81593-Symphyotrichum-dumosum)
-   [Local profile](https://www.fnps.org/plant/symphyotrichum-dumosum#gsc.tab=0)
